summary.intent: Implement the agnostic DatePicker state machine (composite of date text input + calendar in a popover) for ars-ui, covering open/close, date selection, input parse/validation, value↔calendar sync, and ARIA/data attributes. Dependencies DateField (#262) and Calendar (#280) were already merged.
summary.outcome: PR #697 created and pushed to GitHub with 5 commits. The full component was implemented with 127+ tests, 28 snapshots, spec fully synced, and 3 Codex review passes addressed with fixes. Coverage at 100% function / 99.86% line. All CI checks green (the only failure was a label-timing race in the snapshot gate, resolved by re-running the job).

repo: The `Machine` trait requires `type Effect: EffectName` as an associated type — spec §1.7 used string effect names and `use_platform_effects()` closures in core, both of which fail to compile.
Focus effects use `PendingEffect::named(Effect::X)` exclusively in core — adapters execute DOM focus from the named intent. Never `use_platform_effects()` calls in the state machine.
`CalendarDate` has no `PartialOrd`/`Ord` — use `.compare(other) -> Ordering` for min/max checks.
`CalendarDate` fields are `pub(crate)`, accessors are `year()->i32`, `month()->u8`, `day()->u8` (plain ints, no NonZero). `nzu8` does not exist. `new_gregorian(i32, u8, u8) -> Result<...>`.
`ComponentIds` has no public `base_id` field — accessor is `ids.id()`.
`is_date_unavailable` must use `calendar::IsDateUnavailableFn` (= `Callback<dyn Fn(&CalendarDate)->bool + Send+Sync>`), not `fn(&CalendarDate) -> bool`.
`Context` convention: `#[derive(Clone)]` + manual `impl Debug` (skip message fields). Calendar omits `PartialEq` too — `Messages` holds `MessageFn` which is only `PartialEq` via Arc pointer equality.
`Service::new(props, &Env, &Messages)` takes references, not values. `send` is `#[must_use]` → use `drop(svc.send(...))`.
`sync_props_into_ctx` + `Bindable::sync_controlled` is the canonical controlled/uncontrolled re-derivation pattern. Mirrors Calendar exactly.

An `open` Bindable that mirrors State is redundant and dangerous — proptest caught a bug where a controlled→uncontrolled flip revealed stale internal value diverging from State. State must be the single source of truth; the Bindable was removed.

Value-change notification convention (from rating_group): `requested_value` context field + `Effect::ValueChange` named marker. The adapter reads `requested_value` (the requested value, hidden by `get()` in controlled mode) — no Props callback for this.
`Callback::new_ref` is required for higher-ranked lifetime predicates (`for<'a> Fn(&'a T)`). `Callback::new` infers a non-HRTB closure.

`ars-i18n` already had a complete private `date_order(locale) -> DateOrder` (CLDR via ICU4X, browser `Intl` on wasm, `(language, region)` fallback). Both DateField and DatePicker had been reimplementing only the fallback heuristic. The fix was to expose it (`pub`), not add a redundant `IntlBackend` trait method.

`is_component_category` and `is_ars_components_component` in xtask used hyphenated names (`"date-time"`, `"data-display"`) while filesystem dirs and snapshot paths use underscores. This silently skipped two entire categories from the snapshot-count gate.
The snapshot-reviewed label CI gate reads the label live via `gh pr view "$PR_NUMBER" --json labels`, not from the frozen event payload — re-running the job after labeling works.

`ci.yml` `pull_request:` without explicit `types:` defaults to `[opened, synchronize, reopened]` — does NOT include `labeled`, so adding the `snapshot-reviewed` label never re-triggers the check automatically.

code.finding: `CalendarDate::to_iso8601()` returns canonical `YYYY-MM-DD`. Use it for hidden inputs, not `year()/month()/day()` display-calendar fields.

Private `date_order(locale)` function with full CLDR/Intl/fallback derivation already wired — just needed `pub`. Wires ICU4X `icu_date_order` (CLDR probe-date), wasm `js_date_order` (Intl.DateTimeFormat.formatToParts), and cfg-selected `fallback_date_order`.

`is_ars_components_component` listed hyphenated category dirs (`"date-time"`, `"data-display"`); filesystem uses underscores, causing two entire categories to be silently excluded from the snapshot-count gate.
Canonical value-change notification pattern for controlled components: `Effect::ValueChange` named marker + `requested_value` context field. No Props callback — the adapter reads `requested_value`.
Canonical `sync_props_into_ctx` + `Bindable::sync_controlled` pattern for controlled/uncontrolled re-derivation in `SyncProps` transitions.

workflow: Spec code blocks only get `syn` syntax-parsed (not type/borrow checked); blocks can opt out with `rust,no_check`. Spec §1.7 can use illustrative pseudo-code as long as the contracts match reality.
`cargo insta accept` does NOT accept `-p <package>` — it processes all pending snapshots globally.
proptest is essential for Bindable-based state machines — it caught the controlled→uncontrolled open-value divergence bug that unit tests missed entirely.

The `waiting-for-codex-review` workflow loop: post `@codex review`, poll for terminal verdict (👍 or 👀-dropped), triage findings with TDD, push fix, reply inline with commit sha, resolve threads, re-trigger.
`cargo xci-fast` is the mandatory pre-push gate (fmt/check/clippy/unit/integration/spec-compile-snippets/error-variant/snapshot-count/mutual-exclusion).
Using `cd` in a Bash tool call silently shifts the working directory for all subsequent shell state — use absolute paths throughout instead.
Three-pass Codex iteration is normal: pass 1 (initial design issues), pass 2 (second-order effects from pass-1 fixes), pass 3 (cleanup/consistency). Each pass is typically smaller.

friction: Spec §1.7 had ~10 compile-blockers requiring extensive pre-implementation verification: no `type Effect`, fabricated `nzu8`/`NonZero` helpers, `CalendarDate` field access vs accessor methods, no `Ord` on `CalendarDate`, private `base_id`, wrong `IsDateUnavailableFn` type, by-value `Service::new`, missing `on_props_changed`/`initial_effects`.

The redundant `open` Bindable bug took multiple debugging iterations — reading `Bindable`'s internal semantics, writing a fix, then discovering a deeper controlled-event divergence requiring a full redesign (remove the Bindable; State is the source of truth).
Closure lifetime errors in tests: closures as temporaries can't be captured — required `let` bindings.
`cargo insta accept` doesn't accept `-p` — discovered mid-session.
The `snapshot-reviewed` label CI race: the check always runs before the label is applied (label added after `gh pr create`), and `labeled` events don't trigger `ci.yml`. Required manual job re-run.
The `is_component_category` / `is_ars_components_component` underscore/hyphen mismatch silently skipped two whole categories — only discovered during the post-implementation audit Phase 2.
`InputChange` restructuring introduced two sequential bugs (undefined variable, then `Option<Option<_>>` type mismatch) requiring two separate fix iterations.
`Callback::new` inference failure for higher-ranked predicates — `Callback::new_ref` needed, only discoverable by checking sibling test patterns.
17 Codex review findings across 3 passes, several caused by second-order interactions from earlier fixes (e.g. controlled display inconsistency exposed by the `on_open_change` model change).
`cd` in a Bash tool call silently shifted the working directory, causing subsequent absolute-path assumptions to fail until reset.
open_items: CI `pull_request:` trigger lacks `labeled` type — snapshot PRs always need a manual job re-run after adding the `snapshot-reviewed` label. Fix options: add `labeled` to triggers (expensive, re-runs all CI on every label change) or require a CHANGELOG entry instead.
Full `cargo xci` (not just `xci-fast`) recommended before merge — the i18n `date_order` foundation change touches the wasm `web-intl` path, which only nightly wasm-coverage CI fully validates.
Pre-existing `utility/highlight` snapshot orphans block bare `cargo insta test --unreferenced=reject` (feature-gated tests not run in default invocation).
Pre-existing 94 doc warnings in ars-components (crate-wide, none from DatePicker).
Codex pass-3 commit staged but not yet pushed at end of transcript — final push + inline replies + resolve + re-trigger pending.
DateField spec only received a brief §1.7 update — a thorough spec audit for similar drift (like the 10 found in DatePicker spec) is deferred.
Leptos/Dioxus DatePicker adapter tasks are explicitly out of scope and remain as separate follow-on work.
